roles.title: Roles & permissions
roles.subtitle: Define custom roles from panel abilities; create templates assignable by resellers.
roles.assignable_reseller: Resellers may assign this role (within their permission set)

mail_settings.title: Outbound mail (panel notifications)
mail_settings.subtitle: Password resets and system mail. Manage here without SSH or editing .env.
mail_settings.hint: The install script defaults to local Postfix + sendmail. Choose SMTP for SendGrid, corporate relay, etc.
mail_settings.password_unchanged_hint: Leave empty to keep the current password
mail_settings.test_hint: Test uses saved settings; click Save first if you changed the form.
mail_settings.not_persisted_warning: No mail settings are saved in the panel yet. Click Save to persist them before testing or sending real mail (filling the form alone is not enough).
mail_settings.test_disabled_log: The log driver does not send real email; choose sendmail or SMTP and save to run a test.

stack.title: Server packages (PHP & mail)
stack.subtitle: Whitelisted bundles the engine can install via sudo on Debian/Ubuntu.
stack.warning: This runs apt installs; use backups and a maintenance window in production. After Postfix/Dovecot you still need DNS, firewall, and app (.env SMTP) configuration.
stack.empty_hint: If the list is empty, the engine may be down or the API key may not match.

deploy.subtitle: Single flow for git pull, runtime steps and webhook deploy
deploy.wizard_step_4: Run Deploy now for first release
deploy.first_setup_hint: On first setup, repository clone may not exist yet. Save first, then run Deploy now; if it fails, verify repo URL and access credentials.
deploy.runtime_hint_laravel: Laravel recommendation: runtime Laravel/PHP, branch main, and auto deploy enabled.

plugins.subtitle: Install and activate extra capabilities with one click. First release includes migration modules.
plugins.migration_assistant_hint: For cPanel and Plesk, SSH details (and domain on Plesk) are enough first — Auto discover fills the web path and database list. Run preflight and dry-run. If mysql is missing, set MYSQL_CLIENT_PATH or skip mysql preflight.
plugins.migration_card_hint: Configuration and migration steps are managed in a separate modal.
plugins.simple_mode_hint: cPanel / Plesk: server address, SSH user and key are enough. You do not have to type the web root — use Auto discover to resolve the path and database list.
plugins.source_domain_plesk: Site domain (Plesk, e.g. example.com) — required if you connect by IP
plugins.source_domain_optional: Source site domain (optional; defaults to target domain name — used for Plesk/aaPanel path)
plugins.migration_ssh_only_hint: SSH (host, port, user, private key) + target domain is enough. Web root is auto from panel type; use discover or override if needed.
plugins.standard_path_pending: Select target domain or enter source domain
plugins.need_target_or_domain: Select target domain or enter source domain (needed for Plesk/aaPanel path)
plugins.path_optional_hint: Leave empty and run discover, or type manually
plugins.simple_db_hint: After discovery, pick from suggestions below; otherwise type manually. DB user is often the same as the hosting account.
plugins.db_root_hint: You may use the database root user and password on the source; only the correct database name is required.
plugins.skip_db_preflight: Skip mysql client check in preflight (rsync/SSH only)
plugins.wizard_review_hint: Review the key fields before starting.
plugins.preflight_required_ok: Preflight failed. Fix the errors before proceeding to step 3.

ai.subtitle: Smart suggestions for editor, deploy, cron/backup, monitoring and access

terminal.title: SSH terminal
terminal.subtitle: Root login shell on the server (via sudo). Admin only; needs Nginx /engine-ws/ proxy and ENGINE_API_SECRET matching engine jwt_secret.
terminal.warning: Full root access: a wrong command can break the server. Only trusted admins should use this. Set HOSTVIM_TERMINAL_NO_ROOT=1 on the engine service to fall back to the engine user shell.
terminal.use_root_hint: When off, an interactive shell runs as the engine user (www-data). Stored in the signed JWT; no engine restart needed.
terminal.settings_saved_reconnect: Saved. Click Reconnect for a new session.
terminal.warning_limited: Limited privileges (www-data): site files and allowed paths only. Enable root or use SSH for full system administration.

license.subtitle: Activate Pro with the key from our website — no .env needed
license.engine_skip: No local key; engine call skipped
license.ui_hint: Paste the key from your Hostvim account and click Activate. The key is stored encrypted on the server (APP_KEY).
license.activate_hint: Copy the license key from our site and paste it here. You do not need to edit .env.
license.clear_confirm: Remove the stored license key from this panel?

dashboard.detail_cpu_intro: CPU summary and top processes by usage.
dashboard.detail_ram_intro: RAM and swap summary; top memory consumers.
dashboard.detail_disk_intro: Panel disk summary and fullest mount points.
dashboard.top3_disk_hint: Based on system mount points; panel root may differ.
dashboard.system_admin_only: Server resource summary is shown to admins via the engine only.
dashboard.system_engine_hint: If the engine is down, stats will not appear. Check the engine and ENGINE_INTERNAL_KEY.
dashboard.reboot_confirm: Are you sure you want to safely reboot the server? All active sessions and connections will be interrupted.
dashboard.reboot_failed: Reboot request could not be sent.
dashboard.service_shortcuts_hint: Restart frequently used services directly from the dashboard.

panel_self_heal.title: Validate and safely repair panel
panel_self_heal.check_ok: Panel validation passed, no repair needed.
panel_self_heal.confirm_repair: {{count}} issues found. Apply safe panel repair?
panel_self_heal.repair_partial: Panel checked; some steps still need manual action.

domains.title: Domain Management
domains.name: Domain Name
domains.overview_subtitle: Health score, resource usage and deploy status
domains.perf_hint: Safe preset: gzip + static asset cache headers (nginx only)
domains.perf_nginx_only: This preset is supported on nginx only.
domains.perf_confirm_off: Turn performance mode off? (Vhost will be re-applied)
domains.perf_confirm_standard: Enable standard performance mode? (Vhost will be re-applied)
domains.ssl_revoke_confirm: Remove SSL certificate for this domain?
domains.ssl_progress_error: SSL step failed — see message above.
domains.delete_modal_title: Permanently remove this domain and site
domains.delete_modal_lead: Nothing is deleted until you confirm in this dialog. Read the warning before continuing.
domains.delete_warning: This removes the site from the web server, site files, linked databases (MySQL/PostgreSQL when provisioned through the panel), mailboxes, DNS records in the panel, FTP accounts, backup records, and the domain row. This cannot be undone.
domains.logs_empty: No log records found for this site.
domains.confirm_php_change: PHP will change to {{php}}. Are you sure?
domains.confirm_server_change: Web server will change to {{server}}. Are you sure?
domains.confirm_status_change: Site status will change to {{status}}. Continue?
domains.confirm_ssl_issue: Let's Encrypt SSL setup will start. Validation may take a few minutes. Continue?
domains.ssl_loading_hint: SSL validation is running. Keep this tab open to track progress.
domains.traffic_hint: Sampled from Nginx/Apache combined access logs. May be empty right after provisioning.
domains.traffic_no_log: No access log file or no entries yet.

databases.title: Database Management
databases.name: Database Name
databases.ui_url_missing: Web UI URL is not configured on the server:
databases.no_web_ui_for_type: No web UI is configured for this database type.
databases.grant_host_hint: localhost = same server; % = any host (use carefully)
databases.password_rotated: Password rotated — save the new password
databases.import_warning_body: The current database will be dropped and recreated, then your .sql file will be applied. All existing data in this database will be permanently lost. Download an export first if you need a backup.

common.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete this?

ui_mode.tip_1: Security, Monitoring, Cron, and Deploy tools become visible.
ui_mode.tip_2: Changes in admin/technical settings may affect the live system.
ui_mode.tip_3: You can return to Easy Mode anytime for basic tasks.
ui_mode.onboarding_desc: Easy mode shows core features. Advanced mode reveals all technical tools.
ui_mode.advanced_required_desc: This page contains technical/advanced settings. It is hidden in Easy mode.

community.footer_hint: Public Q&A — sign in to participate.
community.home_lead: Browse categories, search topics, and learn from the community.
community.login_to_reply_hint: Sign in with panel access to reply.
community.new_topic_lead: Choose a category and describe your issue clearly.
community.body_placeholder: Explain the problem, what you tried, and errors you see.
community.markdown_hint: Markdown is supported; content is sanitized on display.
community.no_write_permission: Your account does not have permission to post in the community.
community.no_categories_yet: No categories yet. An admin must create at least one category under Admin → Community.
community.admin_sitemap_hint: Submit this sitemap URL to search engines:
community.admin_enable_indexing: Allow search engines to index public topics (robots + sitemap)
community.admin_confirm_delete_topic: Delete this topic and all replies?

files.subtitle: Full path, drag-and-drop, double-click edit, and content search
files.tip_root: Start at site root; use the breadcrumb trail to open folders.
files.tip_drop: Drag files or folders anywhere on this page to upload into the current folder.
files.tip_dblclick: Double-click supported text or code files to open them in the editor.
files.tip_search: Use the search box below (min. 2 characters); tick subfolders to search deeper.
files.name: Name
files.folder_dblclick_hint: Folders: single-click to select, double-click to open.
files.list_error: Could not load file list (engine or site path).
files.drop_zone_hint: Drag files onto this list or use «Upload file» from the «File operations» menu on the right.
files.quick_new_file: New file (with extension, e.g. index.html) — opens in the editor when editable
files.invalid_filename: Invalid name: use letters, digits, dot, hyphen, underscore only; no path separators.
files.file_exists: A file with this name already exists.
files.unsaved_close: You have unsaved changes. Close anyway?
files.cannot_edit: This file type cannot be opened in the editor
files.risky_readonly: This file type is read-only for security reasons.
files.help_intro: In this file manager, the top folder («Site root» in the breadcrumb) is your site’s document root. On most installs this matches **public_html** on the server; put publicly visible files here or in subfolders.
files.help_upload_hint: Drag-and-drop anywhere on this page (or use **Upload**) to add files into the current folder; folders keep their structure. If a name already exists, you can compare sizes and choose overwrite or skip. For WordPress you usually see wp-content, wp-admin, etc. under this root.
files.copy_target_prompt: Target relative path for copied item
files.unzip_archive_prompt: Zip archive relative path to extract
files.unzip_target_prompt: Target directory relative path for extraction
files.perm_backup_na: Not available in the panel yet.
files.recycle_na: Deleted items are moved to the recycle bin.
files.trash_hint: Restore deleted items or delete them permanently.
files.trash_confirm_empty: Empty the recycle bin? This cannot be undone.
files.trash_confirm_delete: Delete this item permanently? This cannot be undone.
files.bulk_trash_fail: {{failed}} of {{total}} item(s) could not be moved to trash
files.paste_conflict_prompt: Target exists. Choose: rename / overwrite / skip
files.paste_conflict_desc: An item with the same name already exists in destination. How should we continue?
files.upload_drop_hint: Drag files or folders here or anywhere on this page to upload to the current folder.
files.upload_conflict_desc: Some files already exist on the server. Compare sizes and choose whether to overwrite them or skip. Non-conflicting files will upload in any case when you confirm.
files.upload_path_blocked: Upload blocked: “{{name}}” is a file but a folder path is required.
files.only_zip_supported: Only .zip archives are supported (RAR/7z not available in the engine).
files.archive_progress_zip_hint: Compressing on the server; keep this tab open.
files.unzip_conflict_desc: Files with the same names already exist in the target folder. Choose one policy for all conflicts.
files.calc_folder_na: Folder size needs a server-side scan; not available yet.

backups.subtitle: Full / files / database backups
backups.restore_unavailable: No engine backup id for this row (take a new backup)
backups.destinations_hint: Save local, S3 or FTP targets
backups.schedules_hint: Create automatic backup plans with cron
backups.remote_restore_missing: Remote restore requires destination and backup set

email.subtitle: Mailboxes for your domains. After the admin installs the mail stack (Roundcube), use https://webmail.yourdomain or any mail client with the ports below.
email.guide_intro: Mailboxes you create here map to the server. This screen manages accounts, not the inbox UI. With the Roundcube bundle, open webmail from the link below; otherwise use Outlook, Apple Mail, or Thunderbird.
email.card_incoming_body: You typically connect with IMAP or POP3. The server hostname is often mail.yourdomain.com or your host’s server name — confirm with your provider.
email.card_outgoing_body: Sending uses SMTP (often port 587 with TLS or 465 with SSL). Your panel login password and mailbox password are different things.
email.card_panel_mail_body: Password resets and system email use the admin-configured outbound mail (SMTP/sendmail); that is separate from these mailboxes.
email.ports_hint: Recommended ports (Hostvim mail stack): IMAPS 993, SMTP submission 587 (STARTTLS), SMTPS 465. IMAP 143 is optional with STARTTLS.
email.auth_full_email_hint: Username is the full email address; password is the mailbox password you set here.
email.deliverability_intro: For best inbox placement: correct MX to this server, add SPF including your sending IP/host, publish DKIM (TXT from OpenDKIM) and a sensible DMARC policy, and ensure reverse DNS (PTR) for the sending IP matches the EHLO hostname. Warm up new IPs gradually.
email.stack_webmail_hint: Server admin: install the “Full mail + Roundcube webmail” bundle under Admin → Server packages so webmail.* and SMTP/IMAP ports are available.
email.dns_hint: For reliable delivery, ensure MX, SPF, and ideally DKIM DNS records are correct (edit on the DNS page).
email.new_password_hint: Leave empty to keep the current password.
email.password_once: We show the new password only once — store it safely.
email.empty_mailboxes: No mailboxes for this domain yet. Create one to get started.
email.forwarders_empty: No forwarders for this domain yet.
email.forwarder_keep_copy: Keep a copy in source mailbox
email.forwarder_domain_mismatch: Source address must belong to selected domain.
email.webmail_note_body: This panel does not display your inbox. Use Outlook, Apple Mail, Thunderbird, or a server-installed webmail such as Roundcube/SOGo; use the address and password you set here.

cron.subtitle: Presets, custom cron expression, and quota summary
cron.info_fields: Format: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week — five fields (same order as Linux crontab).
cron.info_timezone: Times use the panel app timezone: {{tz}} (align with your server/engine).
cron.info_engine: Jobs are stored via the engine; actual execution may still require a server-side cron runner.
cron.info_paths: Use absolute paths in commands (php, wget, your site directory).
cron.examples_body:
# Laravel (change path to your site)
0 * * * *  →  /usr/bin/php /var/www/site/public_html/artisan schedule:run

# WordPress ping
*/15 * * * *  →  wget -q -O - https://example.com/wp-cron.php?doing_wp_cron

# PHP script
0 2 * * *  →  /usr/bin/php /var/www/site/public_html/scripts/backup.php
cron.system_section_subtitle: Auto-created during installation; locked for customer safety.
cron.empty_hint: No tasks yet. Add one above or check your package quota.
cron.custom_fields_hint: The fields below keep the single line in sync.
cron.no_logs: No run logs yet. Use Run now to generate the first record.

monitoring.subtitle: Resource usage, live metrics and service health
monitoring.account_chart_hint: Distribution of domains, databases and mailboxes you use
monitoring.server_hint: Requires admin privileges and a reachable Hostvim Engine
monitoring.admin_only: Server metrics are only available to administrators.
monitoring.history_hint: Values refresh while this page is open
monitoring.load_hint: The first sample may be zero; rates stabilize after a few seconds.

security.subtitle: Firewall, SSH, server hardening, WAF, and attack prevention layers

install.done_log: Installation finished and service check passed.

firewall.quick_stats_hint: Allow/deny counts from recent recorded rules

ssh.intro: SSH access policy and session hardening. The controls below will be managed centrally in Hostvim Pro.
ssh.port_desc: Custom port instead of 22, listen on internal networks only, align with firewall rules.
ssh.root_desc: PermitRootLogin and privileged key policy; optional 2FA pairing (planned).
ssh.password_desc: Disable passwords in favour of keys; tune lockout thresholds.
ssh.keys_desc: Per-server keys, time-bound access, and audit trail.

server.kernel_desc: KSplice / unattended-upgrades style flows and reboot windows (Pro).

website.modsec_intro: ModSecurity with OWASP CRS for Apache filters requests and bodies. On nginx-fronted stacks Apache must run as backend for this module.
website.toggle_hint: When you enable, if packages are missing the server will try to install libapache2-mod-security2 first, then turn the rule engine on.
website.per_site_desc: Per-virtualhost exceptions, sensitive paths, and custom CRS fragments (Pro).
website.crs_desc: False-positive tuning, custom rule bundles, and audit-only mode (Pro).

brute.intro: Fail2ban watches SSH and panel login attempts and temporarily bans IPs after too many failures.
brute.toggle_hint: Enable: if the package is missing, Fail2ban is installed via APT and the service is started.
brute.captcha_desc: Extra panel login protection and geo risk scoring (Pro).

compiler.intro: Disabling compilers on shared hosting makes post-exploit payload builds harder.
compiler.gcc_desc: Disable gcc, make, etc.; allow only in maintenance windows (Pro).
compiler.per_user_desc: Grant compile rights to selected system users (Pro).

attack.intro: Defence in depth across network, application, and session. Advanced items below are planned for Hostvim Pro.
attack.rate_desc: Per-request and per-IP limits, bursts, and location rules (Pro).
attack.ddos_desc: SYN handling, conntrack tuning, and kernel guidance (Pro).
attack.bot_desc: Flag suspicious automation and allow-list good bots (Pro).
attack.geo_desc: Country/ASN rules and real IP behind CDNs (Pro).

mail_cleanup.title: Mail state cleanup
mail_cleanup.warning: Removes engine mail records for domains not present in the panel. Run dry-run first.

installer.subtitle: SMB: one-click scripts · Agency: deploy & Git · Modern stack: guides & tools
installer.page_stability_note: Do not refresh or leave this tab until the install request finishes; in sync queue mode the request may be cut off. If background workers are enabled, the install can continue after you leave — watch the “in progress” banner below.
installer.woocommerce_note: Plugin files go under wp-content/plugins; activate it in WP admin → Plugins after setup.
installer.opencart_automation_scope: Automated step: OpenCart files go to the document root. A password-free DB summary is written to domain/.hostvim/opencart-db-hint.txt (usually outside public_html, not served over HTTP). Finish setup in the OpenCart web installer.
installer.opencart_automation_hint_short: Files are ready; complete the on-site OpenCart installer.
installer.guide_note_not_automated: Note: This app is not installed automatically in this version. The steps below guide you through a safe setup flow.
installer.guide_generic_step_1: First, verify the site root in the file manager (avoid installing into the wrong directory).
installer.guide_generic_step_2: If dependencies are required, run composer/npm from Site tools.
installer.guide_deploy_step_1: In Deploy, pick domain, repository and branch, then run the first deployment (Node/Laravel/Next.js, etc.).
installer.guide_deploy_step_2: Fill build command, env vars and working directory to match your project.
installer.guide_docker_step_1: Docker is not one-click here; use Site tools, SSH or server packages for your container workflow.
installer.guide_laravel_step_1: For Laravel, the recommended web root is usually the `public/` directory.
installer.guide_laravel_step_2: Before installing, make sure the domain document root points to `public/` (otherwise it can be a security risk).
installer.guide_laravel_step_3: Use Site tools → composer install to install dependencies.
installer.guide_laravel_step_4: If you use Deploy, set repo/branch/runtime and run a deploy.
installer.docroot_hint: For Laravel, the web root should usually be `public/`. This safely updates vhost and (if enabled) the PHP-FPM pool config (reversible).
installer.wordpress_automation_scope: Automated step: WordPress files and wp-config.php (with your database credentials) are written; MySQL tables are not created. The setup wizard you see in the browser is expected — finish language, admin user, and site details there.
installer.wordpress_automation_hint_short: Files are ready; complete the on-site setup wizard to create database tables.
installer.running_bg: Installation is in progress (animation active). If background worker is available it continues in background.

tools.title: Site tools
tools.subtitle: Composer / npm in the site document root (engine: manage_site_tools)

billing.title: Billing
billing.subtitle: Packages and subscriptions
billing.payment_methods_hint: This version does not include iyzico. Official TR option is PayTR; Stripe is available for global cards, with optional manual bank transfer.

reseller.subtitle: Sub-accounts and package catalog
reseller.white_label_intro: Logo, colors, hostname and login copy; branded panel for your customers plus optional email footer.
reseller.white_label_hostname_help: The FQDN that points to this panel (e.g. panel.example.com).

onboarding.welcome_body: A short tour of the basics. If your reseller configured custom copy, it appears above.
onboarding.domains_body: Add domains to start hosting and SSL workflows, or skip for now and explore the panel.

system.title: System
system.subtitle: Server resources, services, and web server

settings.title: Settings
settings.two_factor_sub_off: Off by default — enable below if you want extra protection on login.
settings.two_factor_sub_on: Enabled — you will be asked for an app code when signing in.
settings.two_factor_hint_optional: If you enable 2FA, sign-in will ask for an app code. This is optional unless your host turns on a strict server policy (ENFORCE_ADMIN_2FA).
settings.two_factor_hint_enforced: Your host requires 2FA for privileged actions. After you enable 2FA below, use the code at sign-in; some admin APIs need the post-login verified session.
settings.two_factor_active_backup_hint: If you lose your authenticator, use a backup code at sign-in.
settings.two_factor_backup_footer: Save these codes. Each one works only once.
settings.two_factor_disable_hint: Enter your current account password. 2FA, TOTP secret, and backup codes will be removed.
settings.mandatory_2fa_banner: Your host requires two-factor authentication for your role. Complete setup below before using the rest of the panel.
settings.mandatory_password_banner: For security, password change is required after first login. Set a new password below to continue.
settings.password_policy_hint: At least 8 characters; must include uppercase, lowercase, number, and symbol.
settings.branding_hint: Separate logos for customer UI vs admin area. Sidebar picks admin logo on /admin routes and customer logo elsewhere; login uses the customer logo.
settings.branding_limit_hint: Current target limit: around {{kb}} KB. Large images are auto-optimized before upload.
settings.branding_choose_file: Select at least one logo file
settings.branding_413_hint: Upload limit exceeded (413). Lower the limit or increase web server body size limit.

webserver.title: Apache & Nginx Settings
webserver.subtitle: Plesk-like web server configuration (VHost and PHP-FPM pool management)
webserver.engine_hint: Engine must be reachable and expose the webserver settings endpoint.
webserver.php_fpm_socket: PHP-FPM socket (when pool management is off)
webserver.save_hint: Changes are applied in the engine; existing vhost/pool may not be updated immediately.
webserver.ols_include_hint: Add once to httpd_config.conf: include conf/conf.d/hostvim-ols-vhosts.conf; inside HTTP/HTTPS listener blocks include hostvim-ols-http-maps.conf and hostvim-ols-https-maps.conf (see engine openlitespeed package comment).
webserver.ols_not_installed_hint: OpenLiteSpeed does not appear installed. VHost files can still be generated if you enable management.

php_settings.title: PHP Settings
php_settings.subtitle: Edit php.ini and enable/disable PHP extensions (from the ini directives).
php_settings.ini_hint: For module toggles, this page edits extension/zend_extension lines inside the php.ini used by PHP-FPM.

upload_sync.title: Upload limit sync
upload_sync.hint: Saving PHP settings also syncs the file manager upload limit.
upload_sync.config_missing: Unable to read Nginx panel config content.
upload_sync.modal_hint: Running Nginx config test/reload steps. Do not close this page until it completes.
upload_sync.do_not_close: Warning: Do not close or refresh this page until the operation completes.

quick.hint: These controls change directives in php.ini. Click Save php.ini to apply; PHP-FPM may reload depending on your server.

directive.display_errors_hint: Show errors in the browser. Turn off on production sites.
directive.log_errors_hint: Write PHP errors to the error log.
directive.expose_php_hint: Adds X-Powered-By header with PHP version. Safer to disable publicly.
directive.allow_url_fopen_hint: Allow PHP to open remote URLs as files. Disable if you do not need it.
directive.file_uploads_hint: Allow HTTP file uploads (needed for uploads in apps).
directive.memory_limit_hint: Max memory per script. Increase for heavy CMS or imports.
directive.post_max_size_hint: Max POST body size. Should be at least as large as upload_max_filesize.
directive.upload_max_filesize_hint: Max size of a single uploaded file.
directive.max_execution_time_hint: How long a script may run before PHP stops it.
directive.max_input_time_hint: Max time to parse input data (POST, uploads).
directive.max_file_uploads_hint: Maximum number of files in one request.
directive.opcache_enable_hint: Bytecode cache for PHP. Recommended on for production if OPcache is installed.
